A careful reading of an ash-colored owl is said to know the last door a traveler should never open.

The Blue Cloth Over the Empty Cradle

A quiet record built around a blue cloth is placed over an empty cradle so the room will not call for a child who is away.

The Book That Opens to the Reader’s Last Question

A quiet record built around a book opens to the question a reader avoided asking before entering the room.

The Bowl of Salt Left Beneath the Threshold

A quiet record built around families leave a bowl of salt beneath the threshold to keep an unnamed visitor from crossing after dusk.

The Brass Key That Opens Only Empty Rooms

A quiet record built around a brass key opens only empty rooms and refuses any door behind which someone is waiting.

A quiet record built around a legendary bridge has names carved beneath the rail that locals refuse to read aloud.

The Canal That Flows Toward a Missing Sea

A careful reading of a canal is drawn flowing toward a sea that has no place on modern maps.

The Candle That Must Not Be Blown Out Twice

A careful reading of a candle may be blown out once, but a second breath is said to invite the room to remember you.

The City That Appears in the Harbor Reflection

A quiet record built around a city appears in the harbor reflection at low tide, but no skyline matches it above the water.

A careful reading of an attendance sheet includes one extra initial written in the same ink as the teacher column.

A careful reading of a comb from an old tale straightens a river for one morning before the water curls back.

A careful reading of old maps mark a border between two countries, but the land between them is left blank.

A careful reading of one member sees a group icon replaced by a hallway photo while everyone else sees the original image.

A careful reading of crickets grow silent halfway up a hill along a boundary no fence marks.

The Lake That Reflected the Sun Before It Rose

A careful reading of a lake reflects the sun before sunrise because it remembers the first morning before the sky did.

A careful reading of the last locker in a bus terminal clicks open at closing time even when no key has been issued.

A quiet record built around a laundromat dryer returns one clean sock that no customer recognizes, always folded inside the same machine.

A careful reading of a mall escalator pauses until someone steps on the third stair, then resumes as if it recognized the weight.

A quiet record built around a public map pin drifts a few meters after every refresh until users start saving before-and-after images.

A careful reading of a market alley seems to appear only when rainwater covers the painted arrows on the pavement.

A careful reading of an office coffee pot is found full every morning though cameras show the kitchen empty overnight.
